user.title: Home Maintenance Manager
user.description: Create one Home Maintenance Manager instance. Maintenance tasks are added from Configure after setup.

abort.single_instance_allowed: Only one Home Maintenance Manager instance is allowed.

init.title: Maintenance task editor
init.description: {task_count} task(s) are configured. Add, edit, or delete tasks. Choose Save and reload when finished.

data.action: What would you like to do?

confirm_delete.title: Confirm delete
confirm_delete.description: Delete {task_name}? Runtime and history for this task will be removed.

task_name.title: What maintenance are you tracking?
task_name.description: Examples: HVAC Filter Replacement, Pool Filter Cleaning, Hot Tub Water Change, Refrigerator Condenser Cleaning. Task IDs are created automatically.

task_equipment.title: What equipment is this for?
task_equipment.description: Link this maintenance task to a Home Assistant area, device, or entity. This helps with dashboard grouping and future device-specific views.

task_schedule_type.title: When should maintenance occur?
task_schedule_type.description: Choose whether this task is due by calendar time, equipment usage, or a combination of both. Upcoming starts at the warning percent.

task_time_rule.title: Time based schedule
task_time_rule.description: Example: every 30 days, every 6 months, or every 1 year.

task_usage_rule.title: Usage/runtime based schedule
task_usage_rule.description: Choose the entity to monitor and how runtime should be counted. Switches usually use 'on/running'. Power sensors usually use 'above threshold'.

task_last_performed.title: When was it last done?
task_last_performed.description: This sets the starting point so the countdown begins immediately. If unsure, choose Unknown / start tracking today.

task_notifications.title: Notifications and snooze
task_notifications.description: Choose built-in notifications or leave this as automation only if you want to build your own Home Assistant automations from the task sensors. For mobile notifications, select a service discovered from Home Assistant, such as a mobile_app device notify service.

data.advanced_setup: Continue to advanced setup after this page

task_advanced.title: Advanced options
task_advanced.description: Optional NFC behavior and snooze limits. NFC tag IDs can be added later.

task_details.title: Instructions, checklist, parts, and tools
task_details.description: Optional maintenance details. Checklist is a JSON list of text items. Parts is a JSON list such as [{"name": "Filter", "qty": 1}].

task_rules_advanced.title: Advanced rule editor
task_rules_advanced.description: Power-user escape hatch. Most users can keep the generated rules. Edit JSON only if you need multiple custom rules.

import_json.title: Import UI tasks from JSON
import_json.description: Replace all UI-created tasks with this JSON list. YAML-created tasks are managed separately in configuration.yaml.

error.duplicate_task_id: A task with this ID already exists.
